title: Dense, Smoky Middle-Eastern Lamb Tagine
description: A hearty, smoky, and aromatic lamb stew originating from the Middle East, featuring black pepper and a sweet and spicy bhuna paste.

ingredients.name: lamb shoulder, cut into 1-inch cubes
ingredients.notes: preferably with bone for extra flavor

ingredients.name: olive oil
ingredients.notes: or ghee for a richer flavor

ingredients.name: tomato puree
ingredients.notes: made from fresh or canned tomatoes

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat and set aside.
Add the onion and garlic to the pot and cook, stirring occasionally, until the onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the bhuna paste and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the chicken broth, tomato puree, black pepper, and the lamb back to the pot. Bring to a boil, then cover and transfer to the oven.
Bake at 300°F for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Season with salt to taste, then garnish with chopped cilantro if desired.
Serve hot over couscous or rice, if desired.
